key_technique: Extends the TILT encoder-decoder by replacing one-time text+image summation fusion with layer-wise tensor-product fusion applied after every encoder block (with gating, layer norm, dropout). Adds SLED-style chunked sparse attention (block-diagonal mask), nested gradient checkpointing, mixed precision, KV-cache removal and CPU offloading so a single 24GB GPU can train and serve up to 389k input tokens (~500 pages). Trained from T5-Large with extended self-supervised pretraining on CCpdf/OCR-IDL and SFT on 17 DU datasets, plus calibrated confidence scores via per-token min-aggregation.
architecture: Hierarchical Document Transformer based on a T5/U-Net OCR+layout+vision encoder-decoder (822M params). OCR text and bounding boxes drive textual semantics; a small U-Net visual encoder produces image embeddings fused per layer; chunked encoder attention with no overlap and a long-context decoder generate the answer.
limitations: Visual backbone is small (~8M params) and struggles with unusual aspect ratios, slide-heavy or infographic layouts. Performance shows a strong primacy bias toward evidence near the document start (lost-in-the-middle on later pages). Still constrained to ~500 pages and depends on OCR quality; not designed for cross-page agentic reasoning.
fixes: Targets TILT's fragile single-step text/image fusion and its 9k-token ceiling that prevented multi-page DU. Addresses the general MP-VRDU problems of quadratic attention cost and OCR token explosion, while keeping cost low enough for edge/enterprise deployment. Competes directly with hierarchical/long-context baselines like Hi-VT5 and GRAM and very large LVLMs (GPT-4V, Gemini), aiming to match them at sub-billion scale on long business documents.

key_technique: Frozen Qwen2.5-VL-3B-AWQ generator combined with a lightweight (~100M) Pix2Struct-based page retriever. Key novelty is an Adaptive Page Selector that analyses the score distribution: K-means clustering (k=2) splits relevant from irrelevant pages for documents >4 pages with a Top-8 cap, while a probability threshold (0.6) is used for short documents to avoid discarding marginally relevant pages. No fine-tuning of the LVLM; prompt engineering only.
architecture: Retriever-Generator. Pipeline of (i) per-page Pix2Struct encoder with self-attention scoring head, (ii) adaptive page selector, (iii) AWQ-quantized Qwen2.5-VL-3B as the answer LVLM. Operates purely on visual features; OCR-free.
limitations: Retriever requires task-specific fine-tuning per dataset (limits generalization). Cannot answer global, list-of-answers or unanswerable questions well because some pages are always discarded. Hard cap of 8 pages caps recall on multi-evidence questions; cannot recover if all relevant pages are filtered out.
fixes: Directly attacks the rigid Top-K cutoff used by SelfAttnScoring and M3DocRAG, where any retrieval error cascades into a wrong answer. Also addresses the LVLM context-dilution problem on long documents observed in Qwen2.5-VL/InternVL3 baselines, and the heavy compute of cross-encoder retrievers like ColPali. Aligns with the broader MP-VRDU trend of replacing fixed Top-K with adaptive selection (also pursued by ViDoRAG via GMM).

key_technique: Chain-of-Reading: a four-stage trainable CoT paradigm (Task Planning -> Phased & Focused Search -> Cross-modal Evidence Integration -> Synthesized Reasoning & Verification) supervised by a 26k QA CoR-Dataset with explicit reasoning traces. Adds Mask-AR, a self-supervised objective that masks figure/table captions and forces the MLLM to reconstruct them from the visual content, strengthening chart/figure grounding. Three-stage training: LoRA SFT on public DU data, full SFT on CoR+Mask-AR, then DPO alignment.
architecture: Backbone-Centric MLLM Adaptation. Qwen2.5-VL-7B fine-tuned end-to-end; OCR is invoked selectively only after evidence is localized rather than as a preprocessor. No external retriever or agent loop.
limitations: Still bounded by the Qwen2.5-VL context window for very long documents. Requires expensive supervised CoR traces and DPO data. Mask-AR depends on scientific-paper-style figure-caption pairs, which may not transfer to slides/forms. Performance gap remains versus strongest proprietary models (GPT-4o).
fixes: Solves Qwen2.5-VL's information dilution and evidence-localization failure on long PDFs without requiring an external retriever, addressing limitations identified in M3DocRAG/MDocAgent's retrieve-then-read pipelines (decoupled retrieval is brittle). Also targets shallow chart comprehension that hurts Docopilot/Leopard/InternVL3. Provides an end-to-end alternative to fixed RAG and agentic frameworks like SimpleDoc/MDocAgent while preserving interpretability via explicit reasoning traces.

key_technique: Coarse-to-fine retrieval that first uses sentence embeddings then iteratively re-ranks chunks with RankVicuna in groups of m, narrowing to top-3. Pairs this with a multi-page vision encoder built on Pix2Struct: per-page features are augmented with page-position embeddings and merged via attention pooling with a learnable visual query, then fed to LLaMA via an AdapterMLP. LLaMA-Adapter V2-style efficient tuning (LoRA + prefix + bias) avoids full fine-tuning.
architecture: Retriever-Generator. OCR -> text chunks -> coarse-to-fine retrieval -> Pix2Struct encodes selected pages -> attention pooling produces a global multi-page representation -> LLaMA2-7B answers via parameter-efficient tuning.
limitations: Heavy reliance on OCR quality; iterative LLM re-ranking is expensive at inference time. Attention-pooled multi-page representation can wash out fine-grained details when many pages are retained. Frozen Pix2Struct encoder limits visual reading on novel layouts. Attention-pooling itself is a fixed aggregator (cannot dynamically inspect evidence like agentic methods).
fixes: Addresses the single-page limitation of LayoutLM/UDOP/DocFormer and the truncation/noise problems of dense text-only RAG when applied to multi-page DocVQA. Directly improves on Hi-VT5's per-page-then-decoder design by introducing a multi-page vision encoder with cross-page attention pooling. Tackles the general MP-VRDU bottleneck of fitting multiple high-res pages into an LLM context.

key_technique: Dynamically Fusing Visual Context: a lightweight MLP adapter sits on top of a frozen VLM (VisRAG or ColQwen) and predicts gating weights (alpha, beta) over the previous and next page embeddings to produce a residual-fused embedding for the current page. Trained with a list-wise contrastive ranking loss plus a drift regularizer that prevents the fused embedding from straying too far from the original. Initialised with negative bias so the adapter starts as identity mapping for stable training.
architecture: Retriever-Generator (retrieval-side enhancement). Plug-and-play PEFT adapter over frozen VLM page encoders. Operates on cached embedding vectors; no re-indexing of the corpus required.
limitations: Only fuses across a fixed window of three pages (previous/current/next); cannot capture longer-range cross-page context. Requires training the adapter per backbone. Improves retrieval but does not change the downstream generator. Limited by base VLM's per-page representation quality.
fixes: Targets the i.i.d. page-encoding assumption of VisRAG and ColPali/ColQwen, which discards cross-page semantics when tables, paragraphs or sections span page breaks. Avoids the context-dilution problem that arises from naively concatenating neighbouring pages (as a sliding-window text RAG would). Complements graph-based and re-ranking approaches (MoLoRAG, MHier-RAG) by working purely at the embedding level.

key_technique: OCR-free agent built on Qwen2.5-VL-7B that frames MP-DocVQA as sequential evidence aggregation. Begins with a low-resolution thumbnail grid overview of the entire document (annotated with absolute page numbers), then alternates between three actions: retrieval_page (semantic search over unvisited pages via ColQwen), fetch_page (direct index-based fetch of high-res cached visual tokens), and answer. Structured ReAct reasoning template (analysis/relevant_pages/summary) maintains a working memory across steps. Trained with SFT on 9k expert trajectories then GRPO with composite reward (answer correctness, evidence recall, format).
architecture: Agentic Pipeline (open-ended). Single-agent reactive loop over a cached page-token store. Uses ColQwen as external retriever and Qwen2.5-VL as both reasoner and answerer.
limitations: Higher inference latency and GPU memory than fixed RAG due to multi-step iteration. Bounded interaction horizon (T=8) limits very long evidence chains. Performance partially depends on the retriever (degrades modestly with weaker BM25/BGE-Large). GRPO training requires careful reward engineering.
fixes: Addresses the brittleness and rigid Top-K of static RAG (M3DocRAG, VisRAG, VDocRAG) by allowing the model to actively recover from missed retrievals via fetch_page. Also addresses the lost-in-the-middle/scaling problems of end-to-end LVLMs (Qwen2.5-VL, Docopilot, mPLUG-DocOwl2) that cannot fit very long documents. Improves on prompt-based agents (DocAgent, MDocAgent) by being trainable and OCR-free.

key_technique: Multi-agent framework that mimics human reading: extracts a hierarchical XML outline from PDFs (sections, headings, image captions, table titles, with first-sentence hints), then an actor agent uses five tools (search, get_section_content, get_image, get_page_images, get_table_image) to selectively retrieve content. A reviewer agent cross-verifies using complementary modalities; on disagreement a reflection module updates a cumulative task-agnostic memory bank that persists across questions.
architecture: Agentic Pipeline. Closed-source LLMs (GPT-4o / Claude 3.5 / Gemini 2.0) as actor + reviewer + reflection modules. Adobe PDF Extract for outline construction. No model fine-tuning.
limitations: Heavily prompt-engineered and tied to closed-source LLMs; no learned policy. Adobe PDF Extract may fail on scans or unusual layouts. Memory bank is a single growing string that can drift over time. API cost and latency are high. The reviewer uses the same base model, so cognitive blind spots may persist.
fixes: Targets the context-window and irrelevant-context problems of feeding entire documents to LVLMs (Gemini-page-images, GPT-4o-OCR baselines). Specifically beats single-modal RAG (PDFTriage, M3DocRAG) by adding multi-modal cross-verification. Establishes the agentic pattern that later DocDancer, DocLens, MDocAgent, SimpleDoc and Doc-V* refine, exposing the need for trainable open-source agents.

key_technique: First end-to-end trained DocQA agent. Uses MinerU2.5 for high-precision XML outline construction (17 element types with layout/semantic attributes). Provides only two tools: Search (full-text keyword search returning section IDs/snippets) and Read (goal-conditioned multimodal reader powered by an auxiliary VLM, Qwen3-VL-235B by default). Trained on 5k synthesized trajectories generated by an Exploration-then-Synthesis pipeline that explores documents via tool-use to produce challenging multi-hop QA pairs. Loss masks observation tokens to focus learning on agent decisions.
architecture: Agentic Pipeline. Single-agent ReAct loop on Qwen3-4B-Thinking or Qwen3-30B-A3B-Thinking backbones, with an external multimodal Read tool.
limitations: Depends on parsing quality of MinerU2.5; tool design is intentionally minimalist (only Search/Read), which may limit fine-grained spatial queries. Read tool relies on an auxiliary large VLM (Qwen3-VL-235B), so total inference cost is high. Synthetic data quality bounds learned behaviours.
fixes: Addresses DocAgent and SimpleDoc's reliance on closed-source LLMs and prompt-only design by providing the first end-to-end-trained open-source DocQA agent. Tackles the scarcity of high-quality DocQA training data via the Exploration-then-Synthesis pipeline. Improves outline quality over DocAgent's Adobe PDF Extract. Directly competes with MDocAgent, MACT and ViDoRAG by showing a single trained agent matches multi-agent systems.

key_technique: Tool-augmented multi-agent framework with two modules. The Lens Module zooms in: a Page Navigator runs a VLM with OCR text + screenshots over chunked pages (sampled multiple times) to retrieve evidence pages with near-perfect recall (~97% on MMLongBench-Doc); an Element Localizer applies layout detection and crops figures/tables/charts for fine-grained inspection. The Reasoning Module uses a sampling-adjudication strategy: an Answer Sampler generates multiple CoT candidates, an Adjudicator picks the best.
architecture: Agentic Pipeline. Built on Gemini-2.5-Pro (or Flash/Claude-4-Sonnet) plus Tesseract OCR and an external layout detector. Hybrid backbones supported (cheap Flash for navigation, Pro for reasoning) for cost efficiency.
limitations: Depends on closed-source proprietary VLMs and external layout/OCR tools; not open-source end-to-end. Sampling-adjudication multiplies inference cost. Element Localizer offers little gain on text-only questions. Page Navigator must process the full document once, which limits scalability to extremely long corpora.
fixes: Directly addresses the poor evidence-page recall of vector retrievers (MDocAgent/M3DocRAG: 55-71% Recall@10) and the hallucination on unanswerable questions of vanilla LVLMs. Targets the loss of detail when only full-page screenshots are inspected (problem of mPLUG-DocOwl2 and Docopilot). Outperforms SimpleDoc by adding fine-grained visual element extraction and explicit adjudication.

key_technique: Native multi-page MLLM (no RAG) trained on a new 758k-sample Doc-750K dataset (5.2B text tokens, 3.1M images) covering single- and cross-page proxy tasks (abstract/title/caption/experiment writing, translation, multi-image QA). Uses InternVL2 architecture with engineering tricks (multimodal data packing, Ring Attention, Liger Kernel) to extend MLLM context for full-document training and inference.
architecture: Backbone-Centric MLLM Adaptation. InternViT-300M visual encoder + Intern LLM backbone (2B/8B). Document is rendered both as interleaved text-image and multi-image to feed long-context training.
limitations: Performance bound by the LLM's context window even with Ring Attention. Visual tokens still grow linearly with document length, limiting practical scalability beyond several dozen pages. Heavy training cost and dataset construction. Lacks an explicit retrieval/agent fallback when documents exceed capacity.
fixes: Built explicitly to address RAG's fragmented retrieval, multi-stage error accumulation and extra latency (criticism aimed at M3DocRAG/CREAM-style pipelines). Provides the first large-scale high-quality multi-page document QA training corpus, filling a gap noted by InstructDoc, Hi-VT5 and Leopard. Acts as a strong native baseline that retrieval-based methods (Doc-V*, DocLens, MoLoRAG) compare against.

key_technique: EviGRPO: a GRPO variant for multi-page document understanding that adds an evidence-page-aware reward to the standard format and ANLS accuracy rewards. The model must output structured <think>/<evidence_page>/<answer> triplets with per-page T/F relevance flags; the evidence reward is F1 between predicted and gold evidence pages (using F1 instead of accuracy avoids the reward-hacking trap of always predicting all pages irrelevant). Two-stage curriculum: single-page warmup then multi-page training on 4.8k EviBench examples generated by a two-stage MLLM annotation+verification pipeline; releases ArxivFullQA evaluation set.
architecture: Backbone-Centric MLLM Adaptation. Qwen2.5-VL-7B fine-tuned with RL; no external retriever. The model performs both evidence localization and answer generation in one autoregressive pass.
limitations: Limited training data (4.8k examples). Cannot scale to documents whose page count exceeds Qwen2.5-VL's image-token capacity. Reward design assumes evidence-page annotations exist (not always available). Single-pass output means no iterative refinement when evidence is missed.
fixes: Targets the gap that prior visual RL methods (VisualRFT, Vision-R1, Video-R1) ignored multi-page document reasoning. Provides an alternative to retrieval-based pipelines (M3DocRAG, MoLoRAG) by training the MLLM itself to perform coarse-to-fine evidence selection. Complements Doc-V*'s GRPO-based agentic training with a non-agentic, single-pass formulation.

key_technique: Adaptive iterative ReAct-style framework that frames retrieval as InfoNCE-guided mutual-information maximisation. At each step an LLM judge decomposes the residual information gap into sub-queries and the retriever (ColPali or VisRAG) returns candidates whose normalised information gain (gain minus entropy) is greedily maximised. Stops when residual entropy cannot be reduced further.
architecture: Agentic Pipeline. GPT-4o as judge/generator over a multimodal dense retriever. No model training; entirely inference-time prompt orchestration.
limitations: Requires multiple LLM calls per question, increasing latency and API cost. Information-gain estimation is approximated and can be noisy. Heavily dependent on the underlying retriever quality. Not specialised for very long context aggregation.
fixes: Designed against the single-shot retrieval failure of VisRAG/ColPali when queries are under-specified or evidence is scattered across modalities (e.g., needing figures on a different page than the text mention). Influences subsequent iterative agentic frameworks (Doc-V*, DocLens, ViDoRAG, SimpleDoc) by formalising the trade-off between information gain and uncertainty.

key_technique: Compact 2B Small VLM designed for edge deployment. Hierarchical Multimodal Compressor jointly fuses visual patches, OCR text and layout into a fixed 576 tokens per page (5.6x reduction): Local OCR Compression cross-attends visual patches to spatially-aligned OCR tokens; Global Visual Compression then summarises local features into a fixed page representation using global low-res features as queries. Streaming Abstention partitions documents into segments processed sequentially; only text predictions and per-token entropy are stored across segments, then an Uncertainty Calibrator picks the most confident segment answer (with explicit "Not Answerable" abstention training).
architecture: Backbone-Centric MLLM Adaptation. SigLIP2 visual encoder + Qwen2.5-1.5B language backbone. Five-stage curriculum (image-OCR alignment -> image compression -> document compression -> instruction tuning -> streaming abstention) trains compressor + adapter + SLM.
limitations: Compression to 576 tokens/page may lose fine details on dense text-heavy pages. Streaming aggregation is single-pass; cannot revisit earlier segments. Smaller backbone caps reasoning ability versus 7B+ models. Requires multi-stage curriculum data.
fixes: Targets two problems other models leave open: (i) the linear visual-token growth of mPLUG-DocOwl2/Docopilot/InternVL2 that exceeds edge GPU memory, and (ii) the latency overhead of RAG pipelines like InternVL2-RAG (3.5x slower). Builds on GRAM's compression idea but maintains a constant per-page cost. Improves over LayTokenLLM by jointly compressing vision+OCR+layout in one module.

key_technique: Extends a single-page DocFormerv2 encoder-decoder to multi-page by interleaving page-level (intra-page self-attention with shared weights) and document-level (global doc-token-only self-attention) sub-layers in each block. Introduces learnable Doc tokens with a per-attention-head decaying constant bias to force the pretrained encoder to attend to the new global tokens. Optional C-Former (compression transformer with N_c learnable queries) compresses concatenated encoder outputs before the decoder, trading accuracy for latency. Avoids the O((N*K)^2) cost of naive concatenation and avoids extra pretraining.
architecture: Hierarchical Document Transformer. T5-based DocFormerv2 backbone (859M); two-stage block structure (page sub-layer + doc sub-layer); optional C-Former and standard T5 decoder.
limitations: Memory still grows linearly with page count (no retrieval gating). DocFormerv2 backbone is proprietary, hindering reproducibility. C-Former compression hurts accuracy when N_c is small. Still constrained to documents that fit in single-pass encoder+decoder.
fixes: Targets Hi-VT5's lack of cross-page interaction prior to decoding (each page encoded fully independently) and the quadratic cost of LayoutLM/Longformer/BigBird on flat concatenated documents. Demonstrates that local+global attention can be retrofitted to strong single-page backbones without expensive pretraining, an alternative to the OCR-free MLLM trend (Docopilot, mPLUG-DocOwl2).

key_technique: Hierarchical T5-based encoder-decoder. Each page is independently encoded with OCR tokens (with 2D position embeddings), DiT visual patches and the question; M learnable [PAGE] tokens summarise per-page content, similar to BERT's [CLS]. The decoder consumes the concatenation of all pages' [PAGE] tokens to generate the answer. An auxiliary answer-page-prediction head predicts which page contains evidence, providing explainability. Introduces the MP-DocVQA dataset (46k questions over 6k documents up to 20 pages).
architecture: Hierarchical Document Transformer. DiT visual encoder + T5-base text/decoder backbone. OCR-driven semantic + spatial + visual fusion at the page level only; no cross-page interaction in the encoder.
limitations: Pages are encoded fully independently before the decoder, losing cross-page context until late fusion. Cannot scale beyond ~20 pages in practice; truncates inputs. Relies on OCR quality. The single-page-evidence assumption built into MP-DocVQA limits learning of true multi-hop reasoning.
fixes: Establishes the multi-page VRDU problem itself by introducing MP-DocVQA. Solves the fundamental input-truncation problem of single-page LayoutLM/T5/BigBird models while keeping per-page processing tractable. Sets the baseline that GRAM, RM-T5, Arctic-TILT, CREAM and many later models compare against and explicitly try to surpass.

key_technique: Releases InstructDoc, the first large-scale visual instruction-tuning collection of 30 datasets across 12 VDU task clusters with expert-written instructions covering both intent and answer style. Trains InstructDr, which extends BLIP-2 with a Document-former bridging module that fuses OCR text + layout + visual features via cross- and self-attention into m learnable tokens, then projects them into FlanT5's input space via an MLP. Updates only Document-former + projector during training; encodes multiple pages in parallel for multi-page documents.
architecture: Backbone-Centric MLLM Adaptation. CLIP visual encoder + FlanT5/BLIP-2 backbone + Document-former bridging module + small projection FFN.
limitations: Per-page processing with mean-pooling for multi-page inputs limits cross-page reasoning. Most tasks in the dataset are single-page; multi-page coverage (DUDE, SlideVQA) is small. Requires off-the-shelf OCR. CLIP encoder is not document-specialised.
fixes: Addresses the narrow training data of LLaVAR/DocOwl (1-3 task clusters, single template) that prevented zero-shot generalisation across VDU tasks. Tackles the gap that general visual instruction-tuning datasets ignore text-rich documents. Provides the dataset and bridging-module recipe that later models like Leopard and Docopilot extend with multi-image and long-context training.

key_technique: Builds a knowledge graph over the document corpus with passage nodes (text) and structural nodes (pages, tables) plus directed belonging edges. Three KG construction variants: TF-IDF (lexical similarity edges), KNN-ST/MDR (semantic similarity edges, optionally trained MDR-style for next-supporting-fact prediction), and TAGME (Wikipedia-entity overlap). An LLM-based KG traversal agent (instruction-tuned T5 or LLaMA) generates the next likely supporting fact and selects the closest neighbour on the graph, iterating until a budget is met. Structural questions are routed via extracted structure keywords directly to the matching nodes.
architecture: Agentic Pipeline (graph-based). Sparse text retrieval at the seed step; KG traversal performed by an instruction-tuned LM; final answer generated by a separate LLM (e.g., ChatGPT) over the retrieved passages.
limitations: Confined to text-only multi-document QA; ignores fine-grained visual elements (figures, charts). Quality of retrieval is upper-bounded by KG construction; denser graphs increase recall but reduce precision. Requires PDF parsing (Adobe PDF Extract). Not designed for the visually-rich multi-page DocVQA setting that later models target.
fixes: Frames the multi-document/multi-passage QA problem as a graph-traversal task, addressing the inability of single-shot dense retrievers to follow logical chains across documents. Predates and motivates later graph-based RAG work (MoLoRAG, MLDocRAG, MultiDocFusion) and structure-aware retrieval. The structural-question handling foreshadows the layout-as-navigation idea later used by MHier-RAG and DocAgent.

key_technique: Represents the layout of each OCR text segment as a single layout token produced by a layout tokenizer (4D-bbox projector + attention encoder over a learnable query). The layout token is interleaved after its corresponding text segment but shares the first position ID of that segment, so layout tokens consume zero extra position IDs (T-Ratio remains 100% for text). Adds a new pre-training objective NTLP (Next Interleaved Text and Layout Token Prediction) that requires cross-modal prediction between text and layout tokens.
architecture: Backbone-Centric MLLM Adaptation. LLaMA3-8B / Qwen1.5-7B + LoRA, with an injected layout tokenizer. Pure OCR-text+layout input; no visual tokens, so it bypasses high-resolution image encoding entirely.
limitations: No visual modality, so charts/figures are lost. Heavy reliance on OCR quality and bounding boxes. Layout token shares position IDs with text, which can complicate fine-grained spatial reasoning. Requires NTLP pretraining.
fixes: Solves the position-ID waste of prior layout-as-token methods (e.g., DocLLM, He et al. inline {text,Box:[...]}) that consumed thousands of position IDs for layout strings on MP-DocVQA, squeezing the textual learning space. Provides a lightweight alternative to layout-as-modality models (UDOP, DocFormerv2) that need extra encoders, and to OCR-free visual MLLMs (mPLUG-DocOwl2, TextHawk2) whose visual tokens scale with resolution.

key_technique: MLLM specialised for text-rich multi-image tasks. Curates Leopard-Instruct, a 925k instance instruction-tuning dataset including 585k newly collected multi-image samples (multi-chart from Pew, slide decks from SlideShare, etc.) generated by GPT-4o with cross-image reasoning rationales. Introduces an adaptive high-resolution multi-image encoding module that computes per-image sub-image splits based on aspect ratio and resolution under a global token budget, then applies pixel shuffling (4x token compression) before vision-language projection.
architecture: Backbone-Centric MLLM Adaptation. SigLIP-SO400M visual encoder + LLaMA-3.1-8B or Mistral-7B backbone via standard vision-language connector. Trained from LLaVA/Idefics2 base with the Leopard-Instruct mix.
limitations: Despite adaptive encoding, multi-page documents still produce long visual sequences. Pixel-shuffle compression is lossless geometrically but discards per-token granularity. Heavy reliance on GPT-4o-generated synthetic instructions. Limited explicit cross-page agent reasoning.
fixes: Addresses the fact that prior text-rich MLLMs (LLaVAR, mPLUG-DocOwl-1.5, UReader) were optimised for single images and cannot reason across slide decks, multi-chart articles or multi-page documents. Tackles the dual challenge of resolution vs sequence length that hampered Mantis, VILA, Idefics2 and LLaVA-Next-Interleave on text-rich inputs. Complements multi-page VRDU pipelines (Docopilot, mPLUG-DocOwl2) with a recipe for multi-image instruction-tuning.

key_technique: Multi-agent multimodal fusion RAG. Uses a dual-tower retrieval that runs BM25 (sparse text) and VisRAG-Ret (dense visual page retrieval) independently and returns top-K text segments + top-K visual pages. Three Qwen-based agents then collaborate: a Text Filter produces a preliminary text-grounded answer with evidence; a Visual Extractor (a VLM fine-tuned via LoRA on a knowledge-distilled MP-DocVQA dataset) produces a vision-grounded answer; a Modality Fuser performs consistency checking and synthesises the final answer.
architecture: Agentic Pipeline. Backbone agents are Qwen variants; the visual extractor is a LoRA-tuned LVLM. Sparse + dense + visual retrieval combined with multi-agent generation.
limitations: Two parallel retrieval pipelines double indexing cost. Modality fuser still relies on a single LVLM, so cross-modal disagreements may resolve incorrectly. Knowledge-distillation dataset is task-specific (MP-DocVQA), limiting domain transfer.
fixes: Targets the unimodal limitation of VisRAG/ColPali (visual only) and text-RAG with OCR (lossy parsing), which struggle when answers require both modalities. Improves over single-agent RAG (M3DocRAG) by adding evidence-chain comparison across modalities. Aligns with MDocAgent and DocLens in pursuing multi-modal multi-agent fusion, but adds explicit modality-fusion adjudication.

key_technique: ColPali (multi-modal late-interaction visual retriever) encodes every document page into per-patch embeddings; query encoded similarly; MaxSim score selects top-K relevant pages. For open-domain settings, IVF approximate indexing is used to scale to 40k+ pages with sub-2s retrieval. The retrieved page images are passed directly to a multi-modal LM (Qwen2-VL 7B) for answer generation. Introduces the M3DocVQA open-domain benchmark (3.4k+ PDFs, 41k pages, 2.4k multi-hop questions extended from MultimodalQA).
architecture: Retriever-Generator. ColPali (or ColQwen) visual retriever + Qwen2-VL-7B (or Intern/Idefics) generator. No agentic loop, no fine-tuning of the generator.
limitations: Fixed Top-K retrieval; brittle when relevant evidence is missed. ColPali multi-vector embeddings are storage-heavy. Generator is a single forward pass over retrieved pages; cannot revise queries. Performance plateaus around K=4 due to attention dilution.
fixes: Bridges the gap between single-page VQA and text-only RAG (DPR/BM25 + LLM), neither of which preserves visual cues across many pages. Provides the canonical multi-modal page-retrieval baseline that virtually every later RAG/agentic system (MDocAgent, MoLoRAG, ViDoRAG, SimpleDoc, Doc-V*, AVIR) compares against and tries to improve.

key_technique: Procedural scaling: decomposes the document workflow into four specialised agents (Planning, Execution, Judgment, Answer). The planning agent generates analogical relevant plans without execution details; the execution agent picks tools from a tool library and runs each plan step; the judgment agent independently flags incorrect plan or execution steps and routes them back for correction (no self-correction or combined judge+correct); the answer agent synthesises the final answer from both correct and corrected processes. Adds an agent-wise adaptive test-time scaling strategy with mixed reward modelling (per-agent + global outcome) so each agent gets its own scaling regime.
architecture: Agentic Pipeline. Multiple Qwen2.5-VL / MiMo-VL / InternVL3 variants of differing sizes used as the four agents. Tool library accessed during execution. Two-stage RL training pipeline.
limitations: Multi-agent overhead increases inference cost; up to 3 correction rounds per question. Judgment agent only flags but does not correct, so plan/execution agents must learn to repair specific errors. Performance depends on the underlying tool library quality.
fixes: Argues monolithic VLM scaling (bigger Qwen2.5-VL/InternVL3) gives diminishing returns on document-based VQA, motivating procedural scaling. Addresses cognitive overload in single-pass models (Docopilot, mPLUG-DocOwl2) and the lack of internal verification in non-agentic baselines. Improves over MDocAgent/SimpleDoc by separating judgment from correction to avoid cognitive blind spots.

key_technique: Five-stage multi-modal multi-agent pipeline: (1) document pre-processing extracts text via OCR and preserves page images; (2) parallel ColBERT (text) and ColPali (image) retrieval return top-K segments; (3) a general agent gives a preliminary multi-modal answer and a critical agent extracts key textual + visual cues; (4) specialised text and image agents perform focused analysis using the critical cues; (5) a summarising agent integrates all answers into a final response.
architecture: Agentic Pipeline. Five LLM/LVLM agents (typically GPT/Qwen/LLaMA mixes) wrapped around ColBERT (textual retrieval) and ColPali / ColQwen2 (visual retrieval). No fine-tuning.
limitations: Five-agent pipeline is computationally heavy and over-engineered; later work (SimpleDoc) shows similar accuracy with a single agent. Vector retrievers have low evidence-page recall (~71% in the DocLens analysis). Does not iterate retrieval; if both modalities miss, the system fails.
fixes: Targets the inability of pure-text RAG (ColBERT alone) and pure-vision RAG (ColPali alone) to integrate both modalities for questions requiring cross-modal reasoning. Influential in establishing role-specialised multi-agent pipelines for DocQA; later refined by ViDoRAG, M2RAG, MACT, DocLens and SimpleDoc.

key_technique: Hierarchical index combining (i) flattened in-page indexing of small textual chunks (text+table+layout-text) and (ii) topological cross-page indexing built by clustering semantically related text blocks and recursively summarising them with an LLM into a tree. Multi-granularity retrieval: page-level parent-page retrieval (hits a chunk then returns the entire parent page with all visual elements) for multi-modal connection, plus document-level summary retrieval over the tree for long-distance reasoning. Both stages use joint similarity + LLM re-ranking; final answer generated with a CoT prompt and structured-output template.
architecture: Retriever-Generator (graph/tree-augmented). Qwen-VL-Plus visual captioner + Qwen/DeepSeek/GPT generator. OCR-based parsing + LVLM-derived visual descriptions stored in a textual corpus.
limitations: Pre-processing is heavy (clustering, GMM, recursive LLM summarisation). Topological tree must be rebuilt per document. Visual content is mediated through textual descriptions, losing fidelity. Does not natively retrieve images; relies on parent-page heuristic for multi-modal evidence.
fixes: Targets two failure modes of M3DocRAG/MDocAgent: (i) inability to retrieve visual evidence whose surrounding text on the same page is the only handle (multi-modality disconnection) and (ii) inability to aggregate evidence from non-adjacent pages (long-distance reasoning). Sits between pure RAG and agentic methods by enriching the index rather than the inference loop.

key_technique: Multimodal Chunk-Query Graph (MCQG) constructed via MDoc2Query: an LVLM generates multiple answerable query-answer pairs from each modality-specific chunk (paragraphs, figures, tables, equations). Query nodes are linked to their source chunk via Chunk-Query edges and to top-K nearest queries via Query-Query edges (KNN over query embeddings). At inference, the user query retrieves similar generated queries via KNN in vector space, then the linked chunks are aggregated and ranked for the LVLM generator. Storage decouples vector DB (FAISS) from graph DB (Neo4j).
architecture: Retriever-Generator (graph-based). LVLM (Qwen2.5-VL etc.) for query generation and final answer; BGE-m3 text encoder for embeddings; no direct image retrieval (queries serve as semantic anchors).
limitations: Heavy offline cost to generate and embed many queries per chunk. Quality depends entirely on the LVLM's query-generation quality. Visual chunks are accessed only via their queries, which can lose visual fidelity. Graph maintenance complexity for evolving corpora.
fixes: Extends Doc2Query/QCG-RAG to the multimodal setting, addressing the cross-modal heterogeneity and cross-page reasoning failures of standard RAG (M3DocRAG, VisRAG). Provides an alternative to MoLoRAG (page-graph) and MHier-RAG (parent-page index) by anchoring retrieval in synthetic queries rather than chunks or pages.

key_technique: Logic-aware retrieval framework. Builds a page graph by encoding pages with ColPali and connecting pairs whose embedding similarity exceeds a threshold. A VLM (Qwen2.5-VL-3B by default) acts as the retrieval engine and traverses the graph: starting from top-w semantically scored seed pages, it iteratively scores unvisited neighbours by combining a semantic relevance score (from the doc encoder) with a logical relevance score (from the VLM, prompted to reason about question-page logical fit), until a hop budget is reached. Top-K visited pages are passed to an arbitrary LVLM for QA. MoLoRAG+ adds fine-tuning of the VLM on GPT-4o-curated relevance triplets.
architecture: Agentic Pipeline / Retriever-Generator hybrid (graph-based). ColPali for embeddings + Qwen2.5-VL as graph-traversal engine + arbitrary downstream LVLM (Qwen/DeepSeek/LLaVA) for answer generation.
limitations: Graph construction relies on a similarity threshold (sensitive). Multi-hop traversal multiplies VLM inference calls. Logical-relevance scoring is prompt-dependent. Cannot recover from completely missing edges in the page graph.
fixes: Directly addresses the semantic-only retrieval failure of M3DocRAG and MDocAgent, which retrieve pages with overlapping keywords but missing the actual answer. Aligns with KGP in adopting graph-based retrieval but extends it to multi-modal page graphs (KGP is text-only). Influences DFVC's cross-page-context idea by demonstrating logical relevance matters.

key_technique: High-resolution DocCompressor that uses cross-attention with the global low-resolution feature map as queries and the spatially-corresponding sub-image features as keys/values, compressing each high-resolution document image into 324 tokens regardless of resolution while preserving layout-aware groupings. Inserted after the H-Reducer V2T module so compression operates in the LLM's textual feature space. Three-stage training: single-image pretraining, multi-image continue-pretraining, multi-task fine-tuning.
architecture: Backbone-Centric MLLM Adaptation. ViT-L/14 visual encoder (low-resolution) + shape-adaptive cropping + H-Reducer V2T + High-res DocCompressor + mPLUG-Owl2 LLM backbone. OCR-free.
limitations: 324 tokens/page may still under-represent text-dense pages. Compression quality depends on the global feature map's coverage. Training pipeline is multi-stage and data-heavy. Does not retrieve or iterate; performance degrades on documents exceeding context limit.
fixes: Designed against the 1k-3k visual-tokens-per-page cost of UReader/Monkey/InternVL/InternLM-XComposer that prevents multi-page processing. Improves over independent per-crop compression (TokenPacker) and learnable-query compression (Q-former, Resampler) by exploiting layout structure. Sets the OCR-free multi-page baseline that DocSLM, Texthawk2, Leopard and Docopilot compare against.

key_technique: Multimodal chunking pipeline with four stages: (a) Document Parsing (DETR/VGT) detects layout regions and bounding boxes; (b) OCR (Tesseract/EasyOCR/TrOCR) extracts text per region; (c) DSHP-LLM, an instruction-tuned LLM (Llama-3.2-3B / Qwen-2.5-3B / Mistral-8B with LoRA), constructs a Document Hierarchical Tree by inferring header parent-child links and attaching general nodes (tables, figures, text) by spatial order; (d) DFS-based Grouping traverses the tree to produce hierarchical chunks marked by Markdown headers, splitting when a max-length is exceeded.
architecture: Retriever-Generator (chunking-side enhancement). DP+OCR pipeline + DSHP-LLM for hierarchy reconstruction + standard RAG with BGE/E5/BM25 retrievers and LLaMA generator.
limitations: Heavy multi-stage pipeline; DP/OCR errors propagate. DSHP-LLM requires fine-tuning. Hierarchy inference can fail on poorly structured scans. Still text-centric; no native image retrieval.
fixes: Addresses the problem that fixed-length / semantic chunking (LumberChunker, Perplexity chunking, StyleDFS) ignores document hierarchy and breaks evidence across chunk boundaries on long industrial documents. Complements DocAgent's outline construction and MHier-RAG's parent-page retrieval by providing a more accurate hierarchy-construction recipe.

key_technique: End-to-end sparse sampling MLLM for long PDFs with interleaved text and images. A document parser extracts text blocks and figure/table images; a sparse sampler encodes them with a text encoder and a shared image encoder, computes similarity to the user query and selects the top-K most relevant text and image evidences. Trained end-to-end with contrastive loss over positive (annotated evidence) and negative (random) samples, jointly with the LLM. Provides PaperPDF, a bilingual 1.1M-QA dataset with annotated evidence sources.
architecture: Retriever-Generator (end-to-end). Document parser + shared visual encoder (also used by the LLM) + IXC2-VL-4KHD as both text encoder and LLM backbone. Sparse sampler trained jointly with the answerer.
limitations: Requires positive evidence annotations for training. Quality of document parsing bounds performance. Sampler-LLM coupling means swapping LLMs requires retraining. Sparse sampling may miss multi-evidence questions if K is small.
fixes: Directly addresses the dichotomy between pure-text RAG (loses visual info) and pure-vision MLLM (token explosion); positions itself between Hi-VT5/GRAM/Fox/CREAM/DocOwl2-style page-level encoding (still linear) and dense long-context LLMs. Demonstrates that joint training of retriever and generator beats fixed-pipeline RAG (M3DocRAG).

key_technique: Systematic study of RAG for multi-page DocVQA. Textual RAG variant: OCR text is chunked with overlap and tolerance, embedded with bge-en-small-v1.5 (fine-tuned via Multiple-Negatives Ranking), retrieved by cosine similarity, then re-ranked by a cross-encoder; the top-K chunks (text + bounding boxes + image crops) are concatenated and fed to VT5 or Qwen2.5-VL-7B. Visual RAG variant uses Pix2Struct's encoder to embed overlapping vertical patches of page images, retrieved via ColBERT-style late interaction; selected patches are stacked and decoded by Pix2Struct.
architecture: Retriever-Generator. Three model variants: VT5 (textual RAG), Qwen2.5-VL-7B (textual RAG), Pix2Struct (visual RAG). DiT visual encoder used inside VT5 variant; bi-encoder + cross-encoder for textual retrieval.
limitations: Layout-guided chunking strategies were found NOT to help on these datasets (challenges the assumption that layout-aware chunking helps RAG). Visual RAG patch grids may break visual context. Cross-encoder reranking adds latency. Per-dataset fine-tuning of embedder is required.
fixes: Demonstrates that simple RAG can replace concatenate-all-pages baselines and very large LVLMs (Qwen2.5-VL-7B) on MP-DocVQA/DUDE/InfoVQA, addressing the memory-hungry inefficiency of Hi-VT5/Qwen2.5-VL on multi-page inputs. Provides a head-to-head ablation of textual vs visual RAG that complements pure-vision retrievers (M3DocRAG, VDocRAG).

key_technique: Recurrent Memory Transformer adapted to MP-DocVQA. Each page is processed in sequence by a T5 encoder that takes as input the previous page's m memory tokens (default 100), the textual question and the current page's OCR text + DiT visual patches. The encoder updates the memory tokens, which carry context to the next page. After all pages, all per-page memory tokens are concatenated and passed to the T5 decoder for answer generation. Curriculum pre-training adds one page at a time. An answer-page-prediction module provides explainability.
architecture: Hierarchical Document Transformer (recurrent variant). DiT visual encoder + T5-base recurrent encoder + T5 decoder.
limitations: Recurrent processing means later pages have indirect access to early pages only via memory; long documents may suffer from forgetting. Memory size is fixed (100 tokens). Training requires curriculum strategy. Cannot revisit earlier pages once their memory has been overwritten.
fixes: Targets the lack of cross-page context in Hi-VT5 (each page encoded independently before decoding) and the quadratic cost of concatenation-based methods. Provides a low-parameter alternative to GRAM's local-global attention by using recurrence instead of global tokens. Inspired by Transformer-XL and the original RMT for NLP.

key_technique: OCR-free multi-page DocVQA: rendering questions as images and concatenating them on top of page images for Pix2Struct. Two-stage training: (i) train Pix2Struct on positive question-page pairs only (single-page DocVQA); (ii) freeze the encoder and train a Self-Attention Scoring module (a self-attention layer + linear head with dropout) on positive + one randomly sampled negative page per document with MSE loss + label smoothing. At inference, every page is scored independently and Top-1 is decoded by Pix2Struct. Extends MP-DocVQA evaluation to 793-page documents.
architecture: Retriever-Generator. Pix2Struct as both retriever (encoder + scoring head) and generator (encoder + decoder). Pure-vision pipeline with no OCR.
limitations: Strict Top-1 page selection cannot answer questions whose evidence spans multiple pages. Fully visual representation requires sufficient image resolution and may miss small text. Sampling only one negative per document limits training signal. Frozen encoder caps adaptability.
fixes: Demonstrates that single-page Pix2Struct can be extended to arbitrary-length documents with minimal GPU resource usage, addressing Hi-VT5's per-page token cost and OCR dependency. Sets a lightweight baseline for retrieval-then-read that AVIR and DFVC later refine (replacing Top-1 with adaptive selection or fused embeddings).

key_technique: Two-stage framework. Offline: each page is indexed twice as a ColPali visual embedding and a 3-5 sentence VLM-generated semantic summary. Online: dual-cue retrieval first shortlists pages by embedding similarity then asks an LLM to re-rank/filter using the summaries. A single VLM reasoner agent inspects the retrieved pages along with a working memory (initialised with a doc-level summary) and either answers, declares unanswerable, or emits a refined query for another retrieval round (max L iterations). Memory is updated with notes from each iteration.
architecture: Agentic Pipeline. ColQwen-2.5 for visual embeddings + Qwen / Claude / Gemini family for both summarisation and reasoning. Single agent (no role specialisation) over a dual-cue retriever.
limitations: Quality of pre-computed summaries bounds the re-ranker. Iterative loop adds latency. Working memory is text-only; visual evidence not preserved between rounds. Limited tool use compared to DocAgent or DocLens.
fixes: Targets the over-engineered multi-agent pipelines (MDocAgent, ViDoRAG) by showing a single VLM agent with good retrieval can match or beat them. Improves M3DocRAG by iterating retrieval until evidence is sufficient and by re-ranking with semantic summaries instead of pure embedding similarity. Influences Doc-V*, DocLens and DocDancer in promoting iterative single-agent designs.

key_technique: Bilingual LVLM with 16x visual-token compression via TextHawk's two-step resampling+rearrangement (each step reduces tokens by 4x). Visual encoder is unified SigLIP-SO400M reinforced via LVLM co-training to transfer to previously unseen tasks like Chinese OCR and grounding. Adds a detection head with special coordinate tokens (representing a bounding box in 7 tokens vs 25 for a digit string) trained jointly with an L1 loss for grounding. Dynamic high-resolution cropping (up to 72 sub-images / 1.8M pixels at SFT). Pre-trained on ~100M diverse samples.
architecture: Backbone-Centric MLLM Adaptation. Unified SigLIP-SO400M visual encoder + meticulous resampler + Qwen2-7B-Instruct backbone + auxiliary detection head. OCR-free.
limitations: 16x compression may still miss extremely fine text on huge documents. Detection-head grounding requires special coordinate-token vocabulary. No explicit multi-page/agent reasoning module; multi-page support comes from sub-image cropping plus LLM context.
fixes: Targets the high token cost of dual-encoder (CogAgent, MiniGemini) and large-token (UReader, TextMonkey) text-rich LVLMs. Tackles the trade-off between OCR accuracy and grounding ability that competitor LVLMs (GPT-4o, InternVL 1.5) struggle to balance. Provides a strong single-encoder OCR-free backbone alternative to mPLUG-DocOwl2 and DocSLM.

key_technique: LVLM-based dual-encoder retriever (VDocRetriever) that compresses each high-resolution document image into a single dense <EOS>-token embedding, with a generator (VDocGenerator) using the retrieved image directly. Two new self-supervised pre-training tasks transfer LVLM capabilities to retrieval: Representation Compression via Retrieval (RCR) - InfoNCE between OCR-text and image <EOS> embeddings; Representation Compression via Generation (RCG) - generate OCR text from the compressed embedding. LoRA-tunes both retriever and generator on top of Phi-3-Vision. Releases OpenDocVQA, the first open-domain DocVQA collection with multi-hop questions.
architecture: Retriever-Generator. Phi3V-4.2B as backbone for both retriever and generator. Dynamic high-resolution image encoding + projector + LoRA-tuned LLM. Pure-vision retrieval (no OCR at inference).
limitations: Compressing entire image to a single <EOS> embedding loses some fine-grained information. Pre-training requires OCR (used as pseudo-targets). Performance bound by base LVLM backbone's resolution. No iterative or agentic refinement.
fixes: Addresses the limitations of conventional text-based RAG (parsing-induced information loss) and previous visual retrievers like ColPali/VisRAG (no retrieval-specific pre-training). Establishes that LVLMs need retrieval-oriented adaptation to bridge generative pre-training and retrieval. Provides the OpenDocVQA benchmark used by later open-domain methods.

key_technique: Multi-modal hybrid retrieval with adaptive Top-K via Gaussian Mixture Model: the GMM models retrieval-score distribution to dynamically choose how many pages to keep per query, then visual + textual pipelines are fused. Multi-Scale View Generation uses three agents: a Seeker rapidly scans thumbnails and selects relevant images; an Inspector reviews them and provides reflection + preliminary answer; an Answer Agent ensures consistency and gives the final answer. Releases ViDoSeek, the first large-scale collection of unique-answer queries across ~6k images for evaluating RAG over corpora.
architecture: Agentic Pipeline. NV-Embed-V2 + ColQwen2 for retrieval; multiple Qwen / LLaMa / GPT variants as agents. Coarse-to-fine multi-agent generation with thumbnail-then-detail pattern.
limitations: Three-agent pipeline plus GMM-based hybrid retrieval is computationally heavy. GMM fitting may misbehave on very small or very uniform score distributions. Performance hinges on consistency between agents; conflicts can lead to inferior answers.
fixes: Addresses the static Top-K problem of M3DocRAG and the under-utilised reasoning of single-agent RAG. Influences (and is contemporaneous with) AVIR's adaptive selection but uses GMM rather than k-means. Tackles the gap that prior VQA datasets are not built for large-corpus RAG by providing ViDoSeek with unique answers and reference pages.
